scope_boundary: Artifact Graph epistemic trusted computing base fixture only. The fixture accepts three finite trust-base records and rejects six expected-invalid controls for missing roots of trust, same-component verifier laundering, unbounded trust propagation, missing recursion stops, erased outside-TCB residuals, and support promotion from trust-base shape. It is not verifier-correctness evidence, not deployed trust-base behavior, not deployed audit-log durability, not policy-correctness evidence, not open-world receipt faithfulness, not source-interpretation evidence, and not an upward support-state transition.
transition_reason: The epistemic trusted computing base fixture is useful because it makes a record-level trust boundary executable: a verifier route must name a root of trust, bounded delegation rule, recursion stop, verifier-independence state, outside-TCB residuals, and non-claims before it can even be considered for support review, and a well-shaped trust-base record cannot promote support by shape alone. The evidence remains a finite local fixture, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for verifier correctness, deployed trust-base behavior, audit-log durability, policy correctness, open-world receipt faithfulness, source interpretation, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, ASI, or the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable trust-base storage and replay trace
independent verifier-quality review outside the producing component
audit-log durability evidence for the trust-base route
policy-correctness or policy-review evidence for named roots of trust
verifier-independence evidence over real or externally replayed artifacts
open-world receipt-faithfulness or attestation evidence where trust-base claims affect artifact support
source-interpretation adequacy review where source-derived claims are involved
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: missing root of trust is rejected
missing recursion stop condition is rejected
support promotion from trust-base shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: Epistemic trusted computing base validator failure
missing root of trust, same-component verifier laundering, unbounded trust propagation, missing recursion stop, erased outside-TCB residual, or trust-base-shape support promotion accepted as valid

the finite trust-base fixture described as verifier correctness, deployed trust-base behavior, audit-log durability, policy correctness, open-world receipt faithfulness, source interpretation, benchmark, model-quality, safety, ASI, or chapter-core evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed epistemic-TCB claim after deployed or externally reviewable trust-base storage/replay traces, independent verifier-quality review, audit-log durability evidence, policy-review evidence for named roots of trust, verifier-independence evidence, outside-TCB residual lifecycle evidence, receipt-faithfulness evidence where applicable, source-interpretation review where applicable, residuals, non-claims, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: finite epistemic trusted computing base fixture only
three valid synthetic trust-base records only
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent verifier-quality, audit-durability, policy-correctness, source-interpretation, external-project, or external human review
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable trust-base storage/replay trace
no policy-correctness review for named roots of trust
non_claims: does not promote the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim

does not prove verifier correctness, deployed trust-base behavior, deployed audit-log durability, policy correctness, open-world receipt faithfulness, source interpretation, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, deployment safety, or ASI
